people_highlights.name: François Chollet
people_highlights.post:
I wrote Deep Learning with Python to be the definitive guide to how deep learning works and how to best make use of it. Tens of thousands of people got their career start via this book. 120,000 copies sold, and downloaded by millions more.

And now it's free to read online:

people_highlights.name: Anthropic
people_highlights.post:
As AI takes on work humans can't fully check, a capable model could deliberately hold back—and we'd never know. 

New Anthropic Fellows research finds that such a model can be trained to near-full capability using a weaker model as supervisor. 

Read more:

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post: A reminder that telling the AI that it is an expert in a field is no longer helpful in making the AI better at that field.

people_highlights.post: AI-run business experiments are interesting and fun up to the point where they waste the time of humans who haven't opted into the experiments - I think they need to keep their own human operators in the loop for outbound actions that affect other people https://t.co/QluYQ7lwi5

people_highlights.name: Swami Sivasubramanian
people_highlights.post: Agents that perform well at launch don't often stay that way. Models evolve, user behavior shifts, and quality quietly degrades in ways that are hard to spot and harder to fix. Most teams are still relying on manual cycles of reading traces, guessing fixes, and deploying without
